nodes.description: Browsers built or adapted specifically for AI agent use, including headless variants optimised for programmatic navigation.

nodes.description: General-purpose frameworks for building agents and agentic applications.

nodes.description: Builders targeted at non-programmers, typically form- and config-driven with minimal visual wiring.

nodes.description: Builders where flows are expressed as node-and-edge graphs or canvas-based visual programs.

nodes.description: Visual builders oriented around structured, multi-step workflows rather than free-form agents.

nodes.label: Code-Defined
nodes.description: Hosted builders where agents and workflows are primarily defined in code (SDK, DSL, or config-as-code).

nodes.description: General-purpose automation tools and frameworks, including GUI-driven and script-driven flows.

nodes.description: Large-scale simulations of societies and human behaviour using agent populations.

nodes.label: Software Extensions
nodes.description: Extensions and plugins that add agentic capabilities to existing software.

nodes.label: Standards
nodes.description: Proposed and emerging standards across the agentic ecosystem.

nodes.label: Emerging (Ecosystem-Tied)
nodes.description: Emerging standards whose adoption is tied to a specific host ecosystem (e.g. a browser engine or platform vendor) rather than being fully vendor-neutral.

nodes.label: Ratified
nodes.description: Standards with a published specification and active governance.

nodes.description: Opinionated starter kits and templates for kicking off agentic projects.

nodes.description: Tools that synthesise multiple viewpoints or agent outputs into a unified perspective.

nodes.description: Agents and tools built for a single specific task rather than general use.

nodes.description: Utility tools, SDKs and toolkits for building, debugging and extending agentic systems.
